steps.title: Use the but contrast
steps.content: Scholars regularly treat Thoreau's works as representative of transcendentalism. The sentence then says those reasons may seem very convincing, but this characterization risks overlooking subtleties.

steps.title: Choose convincing reasons
steps.content: Choice A suggests showiness, Choice B suggests provocation, and Choice C suggests the reasons already seem inconsistent. The remaining choice means impossible to refute, which fits the initial appearance before the caveat. The answer is D.
